patch.value: Fallback to the exact official Jellyfin OpenAPI document

The authenticated local Jellyfin 10.11.11 server returns HTTP 500 for /api-docs/openapi.json and 404 for alternate local paths. Resolve the public server version, fetch the matching official stable OpenAPI document without forwarding credentials, cache it only under ~/.jellyfin-cli, and expose source provenance in command output.

Local candidates remain preferred; Fallback resolves an exact safe semantic server version from /System/Info/Public; No authentication header is sent to repo.jellyfin.org; Cache is private, versioned, validated, and reusable offline; Doctor, setup validate, schema research, and schema coverage pass against the local 10.11.11 instance
Restore live API research and readiness diagnostics on current Jellyfin.
Agents can inspect the actual server contract even when server-hosted Swagger generation fails.
Exact-version official fallback with explicit source and cache provenance.
Live readiness currently fails solely because the server OpenAPI route returns HTTP 500.
Jellyfin 10.11.11 at a private local URL; jf-cli 2026.7.21-3
Run jf-cli --format json setup validate --require-all; observe /api-docs/openapi.json HTTP 500 and alternate paths 404.
OpenAPI research remains available from an authoritative exact-version source.
All three local OpenAPI candidates fail while connectivity and authentication succeed.
Schema discovery, coverage analysis, and setup readiness gates fail for agents despite an otherwise healthy server.

value.text: Official source selected: https://repo.jellyfin.org/files/openapi/stable/jellyfin-openapi-<version>.json; only strictly validated numeric dotted versions may enter the URL.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: The local 10.11.11 server currently serves 429 operations from /api-docs/openapi.json. The implementation remains intentionally resilient: it prefers the authenticated local specification, validates version-matched official stable fallback sources, never forwards credentials off-host, caches only under the private user data directory, and exposes source provenance.

author: codex-jellyfin

patch.value: The first probe failed transiently; current local discovery succeeds. Unit tests reproduce total local failure and verify exact-version official and cached fallback behavior.

Temporarily make all local OpenAPI candidates unavailable, then run schema or doctor diagnostics; the resolver must fall back safely. Repeated current probes of /api-docs/openapi.json return 356 paths and 429 operations.

A transient HTTP 500 from the local Jellyfin 10.11.11 OpenAPI route exposed a resilience gap. Prefer the local document, but resolve and validate an exact official stable document without forwarding credentials when every local candidate is unavailable; cache only below the private CLI data directory and report provenance.

message: Reconcile issue metadata with repeated live evidence

value.note: Exact-version local official and private-cache resolver
Authoritative research provenance and fallback design

patch.value: Twenty-four resolver tests pass, including local preference, credential isolation, 0600 cache files, cache reuse, and version-mismatch rejection; current live local discovery returns 429 operations.
Schema diagnostics remain available safely when local OpenAPI endpoints fail.
Local-first discovery now falls back only to a version-matched official or private cached document and exposes its source.
Implemented and verified resilient exact-version OpenAPI discovery with credential isolation and private cache provenance.
